title: Capsella grossotriangularis
intro: Capsella grossotriangularis, also known as the rough-fruited shepherds purse, is a species of flowering plant in the Brassicaceae family, closely related to the common shepherd's purse (Capsella bursa-pastoris). The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects, which may provide relief from digestive issues, skin irritations, and respiratory problems. The bioactive constituents of Capsella grossotriangularis include glucosinolates, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its potential health benefits. Capsella grossotriangularis is often used in traditional herbal medicine and can be found in various preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being used in some pharmaceutical applications.
taxonomy: Capsella grossotriangularis is classified within the family Brassicaceae, which is a large and diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as mustards or crucifers. This plant belongs to the genus Capsella, which consists of several species of small annuals with four-petaled flowers. Capsella grossotriangularis is closely related to other members of the genus, such as the shepherd's purse (Capsella bursa-pastoris), which is a more widely distributed and well-known species. The classification of Capsella grossotriangularis within the Brassicaceae family reflects its shared characteristics with other members of this group, including its flower structure and seed morphology. Further study is needed to fully understand the evolutionary relationships within the genus Capsella.
